key_insight: BASE replaces per-candidate Lean formalization with a base-and-edit strategy, formalizing one answer and reusing edits for the rest to cut cost while improving answer selection.
engineering_relevance: This makes Lean-based verification more practical for test-time scaling, reducing autoformalizer calls by around 5x at K=8 without sacrificing selection quality.
method: Formalize one base candidate per problem in Lean.
Train LEANSCRIBE to locate the answer expression and produce a reusable edit function.
Generate the remaining K-1 formal statements by in-place edits instead of fresh autoformalization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a Pareto improvement across all 12 dataset-solver configurations on four benchmarks and three solvers, with about 5x fewer autoformalizer calls at K=8.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliably identifying and editing the answer expression in the base formalization, which may be fragile for complex or ambiguous statements.
paper_title: Formalize Once, Edit the Rest: Efficient Lean-Based Answer Selection for Math Reasoning
